summary.name: Pre-Earnings RSI-Gated Breakout Drift
summary.author: Marcus Anthony

summary.description_full:
The strategy is a short-horizon earnings catalyst breakout system applied to large-cap US equities. Each day it forms a dynamic universe from SPY constituents with an earnings release exactly 7 calendar days ahead, then looks for pre-earnings price dislocations consistent with momentum continuation or downside acceleration. Signals are gated by a daily 14-day RSI filter: positions are only initiated when RSI is outside a neutral band (below 30 or above 60), targeting regimes where sentiment and positioning are already stretched and incremental news flow or positioning ahead of earnings can amplify trends.

Entries are evaluated once per day 60 minutes before the close using daily data: go long if the stock closes above its prior 10-day high, or go short if it breaks below its prior 10-day low, with fixed 1 percent notional per position (no volatility scaling). Exits are checked once per day 60 minutes before the open and positions are liquidated on a 5 percent stop loss, 15 percent take profit, or on/after the scheduled earnings report date to avoid event risk through the announcement. The intended alpha is capturing pre-earnings drift and technical breakouts among liquid index constituents while tightly controlling per-name exposure and limiting exposure to the earnings gap.

raw.name: Pre-Earnings RSI-Gated Breakout Drift

author.name: Marcus Anthony
author.bio:
Quant |

B.Sc in Applied Mathematics from Indiana University - (Summer 2019) |
Minor in Computer Science |

I do a bit of research as well as write custom algorithms for clients.

raw.description:
The strategy is a short-horizon earnings catalyst breakout system applied to large-cap US equities. Each day it forms a dynamic universe from SPY constituents with an earnings release exactly 7 calendar days ahead, then looks for pre-earnings price dislocations consistent with momentum continuation or downside acceleration. Signals are gated by a daily 14-day RSI filter: positions are only initiated when RSI is outside a neutral band (below 30 or above 60), targeting regimes where sentiment and positioning are already stretched and incremental news flow or positioning ahead of earnings can amplify trends.

Entries are evaluated once per day 60 minutes before the close using daily data: go long if the stock closes above its prior 10-day high, or go short if it breaks below its prior 10-day low, with fixed 1 percent notional per position (no volatility scaling). Exits are checked once per day 60 minutes before the open and positions are liquidated on a 5 percent stop loss, 15 percent take profit, or on/after the scheduled earnings report date to avoid event risk through the announcement. The intended alpha is capturing pre-earnings drift and technical breakouts among liquid index constituents while tightly controlling per-name exposure and limiting exposure to the earnings gap.
